items.name: Siren

route.why: Draw from Elvoret during the exam — one fight, one chance

items.notes: Draw-only. If missed, re-drawable from Tri-Point in Ultimecia's Castle — a much harder fight.

items.name: Diablos (Magical Lamp)
items.location: Given by Cid when leaving for Timber

route.why: Use the lamp early — Diablos' Enc-None is the best QoL ability in the game

items.notes: The lamp keeps forever; save before using it.

items.name: Brothers

route.why: Sacred & Minotaur while you have the rental car

items.notes: Note the student ID number on the sword at the entrance. World locks on disc 4.

items.name: Carbuncle

route.why: Draw from the Iguions before rescuing Rinoa

items.notes: Draw-only. Re-drawable from Krysta in Ultimecia's Castle if missed.

items.name: Leviathan

route.why: Draw from NORG during the uprising

items.notes: Draw-only. Re-drawable from Trauma in Ultimecia's Castle if missed.

items.name: Pandemona

route.why: Draw from Fujin while liberating Balamb

items.notes: Draw-only. Re-drawable from Red Giant in Ultimecia's Castle if missed.

items.name: Cerberus

route.why: Optional lobby fight during the battle — easy to run past

items.notes: Defeat it to receive the GF. Re-drawable from Gargantua in Ultimecia's Castle if missed.

items.name: Alexander

route.why: Draw from Edea in the disc-2 finale

items.notes: Draw-only. Re-drawable from Catoblepas in Ultimecia's Castle if missed.

items.name: Odin

route.why: 20-minute timed climb once the Garden is mobile

items.notes: Get him before disc 4 — a certain later duel turns him into something better.

items.name: Tonberry King
items.notes: Defeat ~20 Tonberries, then the King ambushes you. His shop abilities are superb.

items.name: Doomtrain (Solomon Ring)

route.why: Grab the Solomon Ring before the world locks

items.notes: Use the ring with 6× Remedy+, 6× Steel Pipe, 6× Malboro Tentacle in inventory.

items.name: Bahamut

route.why: Ragnarok can land on the research center now

items.notes: Answer the blue light's questions (the third answer is the hidden one), then win the fight.

items.name: Eden
items.location: Deep Sea Research Center — excavation depths

route.why: Draw from Ultima Weapon at the bottom of the dig

items.notes: Manage the steam pressure puzzle on the way down. Also drawable from Tiamat in the castle.

items.name: Cactuar
items.notes: Fight the wandering green sprout — Jumbo Cactuar. Kamikaze and 1000 Needles await.

items.name: Occult Fan I

route.why: One bookshelf check before the exam

items.notes: In the library's rear rack. Garden is unreachable on disc 4.

items.name: Occult Fan II
items.notes: Buy it from the pub. Also involves the sad card-playing owner upstairs.

items.name: Occult Fan III
items.notes: Tied to the vase quest's conclusion — verify the exact handover in play.

items.name: Occult Fan IV
items.notes: From the aide near the presidential palace after the Esthar story opens up.

items.name: Timber Maniacs issues
items.notes: Timber, Dollet pub, Balamb hotel, FH, Trabia, Shumi, White SeeD ship and more — several rooms lock on disc 4. They feed Selphie's website and the Sir Laguna lore.

items.name: Pet Pals Vol. 1

route.why: Check Rinoa's bed on the train — gone when the mission ends

items.notes: Angelo's first trick magazine. The rest are purchasable later; this one is not.

items.name: Combat King 001–005
items.notes: 001 in D-District Prison and 002 in occupied Balamb are effectively one-shot windows — grab them during those beats. 003 from Zell's library admirer, 004 from an Esthar soldier, 005 on the Lunar Base.

items.name: Weapons Monthly issues
items.notes: Remodel blueprints. The March issue (ultimate weapons) is the one that matters — Balamb Garden 2F classroom panel among others.

items.name: Queen of Cards questline
items.notes: Deliberately lose specific rare cards to move her around the world; her father then creates new rares (Kiros, Irvine, Chubby Chocobo, Doomtrain, Phoenix). Every lost card is winnable back.

items.name: CC Group card club
items.notes: Beat Jack through King inside the Garden before disc 4 — the club then joins the Ragnarok holding nearly every rare card.

items.name: Rare card sweep
items.notes: Win the one-holder rares before the world locks: Seifer (Cid), Rinoa (General Caraway), Edea (herself, disc 3), Laguna (Ellone), Squall (Laguna). Most others migrate to the CC Group on disc 4.

items.name: Girl Next Door → Zone

route.why: Sell Zone his magazine aboard the ship — the visit doesn't repeat

items.notes: Pick the magazine up in Timber's Maniacs building first. Reward: Shiva card via Rename Card chain? Verify — sources differ; treat the delivery as the one-shot.

items.name: Winhill vase shards
items.notes: Reassemble the mayor's vase — four pieces around town; one involves the chicobo and one the flower field.

items.name: Obel Lake mystery
items.notes: Talk to the shadow in the lake, chase Mr. Monkey and the rock riddles across the continents. Ends in Three Stars and a Luck-J Scroll.

items.name: Shumi Village artisan quest
items.notes: The statue of Laguna, the Moomba, and the attendant's errands. Long, odd, worth it.

items.name: Master Fisherman
items.notes: The old man fishing off the dock — follow his story through FH's shops for his reward.

items.name: PuPu (UFO quest)
items.notes: Encounter the UFO at Winhill, Mandy Beach, Heath Peninsula and Trabia's glacier, then land at the crater and hand PuPu five Elixirs for its card. Do not attack it.

items.name: Lion Heart (Squall)

route.why: Remodel before disc 4 — no junk shops after the world locks

items.notes: Adamantine ×1, Dragon Fang ×4, Pulse Ammo ×12. Famously possible on disc 1 via Elnoyle cards and T-Rexaur mods.

items.name: Everyone's final weapons
items.notes: Ehrgeiz, Exeter, Shooting Star, Strange Vision, Crescent Wish — remodel the party's best before junk shops become unreachable.

items.name: Chocobo forests & Chicobo
items.notes: Solve each forest with the ChocoZiner; the final forest yields Mog's Amulet and the Chicobo card. Forests stay reachable on disc 4.

items.name: Omega Weapon
items.notes: Ring the bell with one party, sprint to the chapel with the other. The true superboss.
